opening_phrases: In this study, we investigated how humans detect changes in the environments and the neural mechanisms that contribute to how we might under- and overreact in our judgments
Combining a novel behavioral paradigm with computational modeling and fMRI
Regime shifts—the transition from one state of the world to another—are present in many daily situations
The world constantly changes, with the underlying state of the world shifting from one regime to another
determining whether a regime has changed is usually not obvious, as signals are noisy and reflective of the volatility of the environment
Respiration is governed by a widespread network of cortical and subcortical structures
This complex communication between the brain and lungs is altered in pathological conditions
We investigated if there was any evidence for cortical coupling with respiration in newborn humans and whether it relates to apnoea
